### Format Sandbox Timeout Error (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.3/errors/page.mdx

A function to format sandbox timeout errors. It takes a message string and returns a TimeoutError object. This is useful for standardizing how timeout errors are represented.

```typescript
function formatSandboxTimeoutError(message: string): TimeoutError {
  return new TimeoutError(message);
}
// Defined in errors.ts:2
```

### Check Sandbox Status (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.2.0/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Verifies if a sandbox instance is currently running. This method accepts an optional request timeout for the API call. It returns a boolean value indicating the running status.

```python
def is_running(request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> bool:
    # ... implementation details ...
    pass

# Example Usage:
sandbox = Sandbox()
print(f"Sandbox running: {sandbox.is_running()}")
sandbox.kill()
print(f"Sandbox running after kill: {sandbox.is_running()}")
```

### Define SandboxError Class (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.2/errors/page.mdx

Defines the base SandboxError class, intended for general sandbox-related errors. It serves as a parent class for more specific error types. Errors are instantiated with a message string.

```typescript
class SandboxError extends Error {
  constructor(message: any) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "SandboxError";
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Check if an E2B Sandbox is running

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v2.0.1/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Verifies the operational status of an E2B sandbox. It can optionally accept a request timeout in seconds. This is useful for ensuring a sandbox is active before attempting operations.

```python
sandbox = Sandbox.create()
is_running = sandbox.is_running(request_timeout=5.0)
print(f"Sandbox running: {is_running}")
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: Extract Sandbox Error from Response

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.5.0/messaging/page.mdx

The `extractError()` function takes a `Response` object and asynchronously returns a `SandboxError` if an error occurred during sandbox execution, or `undefined` otherwise. This is crucial for handling and reporting errors.

```typescript
function extractError(res: Response): Promise<undefined | SandboxError>
```

--------------------------------

### Check Sandbox Running Status (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.2.1/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Asynchronously checks if the sandbox is currently running. It accepts an optional request timeout in seconds.

```python
from e2b import Sandbox

async def check_running_status():
    sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
    is_running = await sandbox.is_running()
    print(f"Sandbox is running: {is_running}")
    await sandbox.close()
```

--------------------------------

### Create Execution Error Object in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.5.0/messaging/page.mdx

Initializes an `ExecutionError` object to capture details about an error that occurred during cell execution. It stores the error's name, value, and a detailed traceback, crucial for debugging.

```typescript
new ExecutionError(
   name: string, 
   value: string, 
   traceback: string): ExecutionError
```

--------------------------------

### Check if a sandbox is currently running

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.3.1/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Verifies the running status of a sandbox instance. This is useful for determining if operations can be performed or if a sandbox needs to be restarted. An optional request timeout can be specified.

```python
from e2b import Sandbox

sandbox = Sandbox()
if sandbox.is_running():
    print('Sandbox is running.')
else:
    print('Sandbox is not running.')
```

--------------------------------

### Define AuthenticationError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.7/errors/page.mdx

Thrown when authentication fails. This error class is part of the E2B SDK's custom error hierarchy for handling specific failure scenarios. It accepts a message parameter for detailed error information.

```typescript
class AuthenticationError extends Error {
  constructor(message: any) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "AuthenticationError";
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Instance - Check Status

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.3.2/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Checks if the sandbox is currently running.

```APIDOC
## Sandbox.is_running

### Description
Checks if the sandbox is running.

### Method
Instance Method

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds

### Returns
- **bool** - `True` if the sandbox is running, `False` otherwise.

### Usage
```python
from e2b import Sandbox

sandbox = Sandbox()

if sandbox.is_running():
    print("Sandbox is running.")
else:
    print("Sandbox is not running.")

# Example after killing the sandbox
sandbox.kill()
sandbox.is_running() # Returns False
```
```

--------------------------------

### Enable Auto-Pause on Sandbox Creation - JavaScript and Python

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox/persistence/page.mdx

Details how to enable the auto-pause feature when creating a sandbox using the beta method `Sandbox.betaCreate()` or `Sandbox.beta_create()`. This allows sandboxes to automatically pause after a period of inactivity, preserving state. The default inactivity timeout is 10 minutes, which can be customized.

```javascript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

// Create sandbox with auto-pause enabled (Beta)
const sandbox = await Sandbox.betaCreate({
    autoPause: true, // Auto-pause after the sandbox times out
    timeoutMs: 10 * 60 * 1000 // Optional: change the default timeout (10 minutes)
})
```

```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox
# Create sandbox with auto-pause enabled (Beta)
sandbox = Sandbox.beta_create(
    auto_pause=True  # Auto-pause after the sandbox times out
    timeout=10 * 60, # Optional: change the default timeout (10 minutes)
)
```

### Define SandboxError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.4/errors/page.mdx

SandboxError is the base class for all sandbox-related errors within the E2B SDK. It can be instantiated with an optional message and stack trace. This class serves as a common ancestor for more specific sandbox errors, facilitating centralized error handling.

```typescript
class SandboxError {
  constructor(message?: string, stackTrace?: string)
}
```

### Define AuthenticationError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.5.0/errors/page.mdx

Thrown when authentication fails. This error class is part of the E2B SDK's error hierarchy.

```typescript
class AuthenticationError {
  message: any;

  constructor(message: any) {
    this.message = message;
  }
}
```

### Sandbox Status Check

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.9.1/sandbox/page.mdx

Checks if the sandbox is currently running.

```APIDOC
## GET /sandbox/{sandboxId}/status

### Description
Checks if the sandbox is running.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/sandbox/{sandboxId}/status

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **sandboxId** (string) - Required - The ID of the sandbox.

#### Query Parameters
- **requestTimeoutMs** (number) - Optional - Connection options, such as request timeout.

### Request Example
```ts
import { Sandbox } from 'e2b'

const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
const isRunning = await sandbox.isRunning()
console.log(isRunning);

await sandbox.kill()
const isStillRunning = await sandbox.isRunning()
console.log(isStillRunning);
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **isRunning** (boolean) - `true` if the sandbox is running, `false` otherwise.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "isRunning": true
}
```
```

--------------------------------

Represents an error when a requested resource cannot be found. This error extends SandboxError and takes a string message as input.

```typescript

  constructor(message: string) {
    super(message);
  }
}
```

# Example Usage:

### ExecutionError Data Structure

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-python-sdk/v1.0.4/sandbox/page.mdx

Represents an error during code execution, including the error name, value, and traceback. Provides a method to convert the error to a JSON string.

```python
@dataclass
class ExecutionError():
    """Represents an error that occurred during the execution of a cell.
The error contains the name of the error, the value of the error, and the traceback."""
    # Name of the error.
    name: str
    # Value of the error.
    value: str
    # The raw traceback of the error.
    traceback: str

    def to_json(self) -> str:
        """Returns the JSON representation of the Error object."""
        pass
```
